For 'not found' on reply/delete, the route handler does an upfront getById null check returning 404 directly — ValidationError-from-action would map to 400 instead.

MailStore.deleteById row-level pattern (overstory-2740): deleteById(id): boolean returns true iff a row was deleted, using the bun:sqlite stmt.run() result.changes count. Wraps the DELETE in try/catch and throws MailError with messageId+cause on failure, mirroring insert(). Sits alongside purge(options) — purge handles bulk/criteria delete, deleteById handles the row-level case for HTTP DELETE /api/mail/:id. MailClient.deleteById is a thin proxy to the store.

REST endpoints driving the headless coordinator (overstory-82b4): src/commands/serve/coordinator-actions.ts thin wrappers (sendToCoordinator, askCoordinatorAction, getCoordinatorState, startCoordinatorHeadless, stopCoordinator) consumed by route handlers in src/commands/serve/rest.ts. Actions accept CoordinatorActionDeps {projectRoot, optional _sessionStore/_mailStore for DI} and open short-lived stores when no overrides are provided. Tmux-only sessions (tmuxSession !== '') without a registered HeadlessClaudeConnection trigger ConflictError mapped to HTTP 409.

Headless coordinator start path (overstory-82b4): startCoordinatorSession in src/commands/coordinator.ts accepts optional headless: boolean option. When true, the function early-returns after identity creation, calls runtime.buildDirectSpawn() + spawnHeadlessAgent({agentName: 'coordinator'}), writes the initial stdin prompt via buildInitialHeadlessPrompt (agentDef + pending dispatch mail + beacon), and persists session row with tmuxSession: ''. ValidationError is thrown when runtime lacks buildDirectSpawn. The CLI 'ov coordinator start' command does NOT yet pass headless—stays tmux-only.

name: ov serve REST POST body parsing
description: POST /api/* handlers in src/commands/serve/rest.ts use readJsonBody<T>(req, validate) helper that catches JSON parse errors as ValidationError('Invalid JSON body') (mapped to HTTP 400) and accepts a validate callback. asObject/requireString/optionalString/optionalTimeoutSec compose validation. Handler context (HandlerContext) threads coordinatorActions + coordinatorActionDeps through every handler — overrides via RestApiDeps._coordinatorActions allow test stubs without re-registering routes for read-only paths.

content: ov sling --base-branch chaining for sequential builder swarms (overstory-d108): when a lead decomposes into ordered builders where builder 2 imports builder 1's API, spawn builder 2 with --base-branch pointing at builder 1's branch (overstory/<builder1-name>/<task-id>). Builder 2's worktree starts with builder 1's commits applied, so imports resolve and bun install/typecheck pass without waiting for the coordinator to merge builder 1 first. The coordinator's FIFO merge queue still serializes the merges (foundations first, then integration). This is the canonical pattern for layered UI work where deps + primitives + hand-vendored shadcn (Builder 1) must precede route integration (Builder 2). Tested on overstory-d108: 17-file foundations builder + 6-file integration builder, both clean lint/typecheck, 0 test regressions, no blocking on coordinator merge.

ui/ shadcn primitive hand-vendor extends to sonner/cmdk/dialog/dropdown-menu (overstory-d108): the existing hand-vendor pattern (mx-2acfd3, mx-b9551e — table.tsx, card.tsx, badge.tsx) scales cleanly to richer shadcn primitives. For sonner: thin Toaster wrapper at ui/src/components/ui/sonner.tsx that reads useTheme() to set sonner's theme prop. For cmdk-backed Command primitive: ui/src/components/ui/command.tsx with Command, CommandDialog (wraps Radix Dialog), CommandInput, CommandList, CommandEmpty, CommandGroup, CommandItem, CommandSeparator, CommandShortcut — exports alphabetized for biome organizeImports. For Radix dialog: ui/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx with Dialog, DialogTrigger, DialogContent, DialogHeader, DialogFooter, DialogTitle, DialogDescription, DialogClose. For Radix dropdown-menu: ui/src/components/ui/dropdown-menu.tsx with the minimal subset (DropdownMenu, DropdownMenuTrigger, DropdownMenuContent, DropdownMenuItem, DropdownMenuSeparator, DropdownMenuLabel). All match data-slot=name + cn(...) shape. Required deps in ui/package.json: sonner, cmdk, @radix-ui/react-dialog, @radix-ui/react-dropdown-menu. NEVER run bunx shadcn add — interactive prompts stall.

ui/ Cmd+K command-palette composer-aware shortcut guard (overstory-d108): global keydown listener for Cmd/Ctrl+K must skip when focus is inside a textarea, input, or contentEditable element so it does not hijack composer typing. Pattern: const tag = el.tagName.toLowerCase(); if (tag === 'textarea' || tag === 'input') return true; if ((el as HTMLElement).isContentEditable) return true. Listener attached to window in a useEffect, calls preventDefault before toggling palette open state. Provider exposes { open, setOpen } via a CommandPaletteContext so other leads can register additional commands or trigger the palette programmatically. Recent runs use useQuery(['runs']) with enabled: open so the query refetches only when the palette is visible — React Query dedupes with the existing fetchRuns(50) usage in Layout/Home. Reference impl: ui/src/components/command-palette.tsx.

ui/ global WS-status registry pattern (overstory-d108): top-nav connection indicator needs aggregate status across all mounted useWebSocket hooks, not just one. Solution: ui/src/lib/ws-status.tsx exports WsStatusProvider (context with a Map<string, WsStatus> ref + aggregated state), useGlobalWsStatus() returning 'open' | 'connecting' | 'closed' | 'idle' (idle=no sockets), and an internal useRegisterWsStatus(status: WsStatus | null) hook. Aggregation rule: any open=open; else any connecting=connecting; else any closed=closed; else idle. ui/src/lib/ws.ts adds ONE useRegisterWsStatus(url===null ? null : status) call inside useWebSocket — public { status, ws } return signature unchanged. Pass null for inactive sockets so they don't pollute the aggregate. If no provider in tree, the registration is a no-op (useContext returns null) — useful for tests. Mount WsStatusProvider above QueryClientProvider/BrowserRouter at the app root.

404 vs 400 in REST actions: when a 'not found' case must surface as HTTP 404 (delete, reply on missing message), keep an upfront mailStore.getById() null-check at the route handler layer. Action helper still throws ValidationError for safety when called programmatically — both layers needed because ValidationError maps to 400, not 404.

name: rest-sibling-methods-two-pass
description: REST router: two-pass match (path+method first, fallback 405 only if path matched) lets sibling methods coexist on the same path pattern (GET vs DELETE on /api/mail/:id). Keeps existing 405-on-known-path semantics.

name: thin-rest-handlers-pure-actions
description: Operator-write REST handlers: keep route handlers thin (parseJsonBody → action → apiJson) and put validation+dispatch in pure helpers (e.g., src/commands/serve/mail-actions.ts). Helpers throw OverstoryError subclasses; the route dispatcher maps to HTTP. Helpers are unit-testable without spinning a server.

content: ui combobox/popover explicit dismissal pattern (overstory-0d64): for a value-derived popover (e.g. ui/src/routes/coordinator/Composer.tsx slash menu where showSlashMenu = isSlashOnly(value)), explicit dismissal on Escape/click-outside/blur/submit/select requires (1) a dismissed boolean state, (2) showSlashMenu = useMemo(() => derivedFromValue && !dismissed, [...]), (3) onMouseDown.preventDefault on menu items so textarea does not blur before click registers, (4) useEffect mousedown listener on document gated on showSlashMenu for click-outside, (5) setDismissed(true) at top of submit handler so onChange('') after send does not reopen the menu, (6) setDismissed(false) in textarea's onChange so user typing always resets dismissal — matches standard combobox UX. The mousedown.preventDefault on menu items is the critical detail: without it, blur fires before click and the menu hides before the selection runs.

name: ov serve auto-build + --dev wiring pattern
description: ov serve auto-builds ui/dist when stale (mtime sweep across ui/src/**, root config files) and gates on opts.dev to run startDevServer instead, which spawns ui/dev-server.ts as a Bun HMR proxy that forwards /api/* and /ws back to the main bound port. Inject _ensureUiBuild / _startDevServer / _skipAutoBuild deps to short-circuit in tests via thrown sentinel errors before runServe blocks.

content: When testing runServe() (which blocks indefinitely on a never-resolving promise), inject deps with sentinel errors (e.g. throw new Error('__halt__') from _ensureUiBuild or _startDevServer) so runServe rejects before binding signal handlers. Use port:0 when createServeServer is reached so the leaked bound server doesn't collide across tests.

name: ov serve auto-build + dev proxy pattern (overstory-55e6)
description: ov serve auto-build runs ensureUiBuild() before binding the port (gated on !opts.dev && !deps._skipAutoBuild); ensureUiBuild walks ui/src + tracked root files (index.html, build.ts, package.json, tsconfig.app.json, tsconfig.json, components.json) and compares mtime to ui/dist/index.html; runs bun install only when ui/node_modules missing. --dev flag spawns ui/dev-server.ts (Bun.serve with HTML route + bidirectional /api+/ws proxy back to main port) so UI client code uses unchanged relative URLs. Avoids ui/src/lib/* changes — proxy on the dev side rather than CORS + UI dev detection. Subprocess shutdown: SIGTERM with 5s SIGKILL fallback, runs before stopMailInjectors/stopBroadcaster.

content: ov sling resume-flag threading: in the headless branch (src/commands/sling.ts ~line 985), read store.getByName(name)?.claudeSessionId before runtime.buildDirectSpawn and pass it as resumeSessionId. The AgentSession upsert at the end of the headless branch MUST include claudeSessionId: priorClaudeSessionId — otherwise the upsert clobbers the column to NULL (see src/sessions/store.ts:289 ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE) and the tailer cannot detect resume mismatches on the next event.

name: tailer-session-id-capture
description: Tailer captures runtime session_id from stream-json system events. The watchdog tailer (src/events/tailer.ts) opens a SessionStore handle on start, then on the first system event with a snake_case session_id, calls SessionStore.updateClaudeSessionId(agent, observed). If a prior claudeSessionId is set and differs from observed, a resume-mismatch warning fires (DI hook _onResumeMismatch in tests, process.stderr in production). Pinned via per-tailer flag so subsequent system events are ignored.

content: ov serve mail injection: SessionStore-driven discovery (overstory-2cf9): installMailInjectors in src/commands/serve.ts no longer scans .overstory/agents/* for FIFO files (the FIFO module is gone). It opens SessionStore on each rescan and lists sessions whose state ∈ {booting, working, stalled} AND whose capability isTaskScopedCapability(). Loops auto-stop when a session transitions to completed/zombie on the next 5s rescan tick. The dispatch parameter (config + manifest) is REQUIRED for any agent to receive mail under Phase 3 — when omitted (e.g., manifest fails to load), installMailInjectors returns a no-op stop and agents get NO mail injection. There is no FIFO fallback.

ov nudge delivery preference order (overstory-2cf9): src/commands/nudge.ts:nudgeAgent prefers in-process RuntimeConnection (Sapling RPC) → spawn-per-turn dispatch via runTurn → tmux send-keys, in that order. The order matters because Sapling agents register a HeadlessClaudeConnection-sibling RPC handle; a runTurn spawn would ignore the live process. Task-scoped Claude has no RuntimeConnection between turns, so getConnection() returns undefined and tryNudgeViaTurnRunner is invoked — which in turn calls runTurn synchronously. Tmux is the third path for interactive coordinator/orchestrator/monitor. Updates: see mx-17830a (runtime-agnostic order), mx-0c75cc (HeadlessClaudeConnection.nudge).

name: ov-serve-ui-dist-package-fallback
description: ov serve resolveUiDistPath() prefers <projectRoot>/ui/dist and falls back to the prebuilt assets shipped inside @os-eco/overstory-cli (overstory-916d). Pair with ensureUiBuild's no-op branch when ui/src is absent so a fresh ov init in a project without a ui/ workspace serves UI from the package's bundled dist instead of crashing the build helper.

content: ov sling --recover bypasses the workable-status check (sling.ts isTaskWorkable) so coordinators can re-dispatch a fresh agent against a task whose prior owner exited. nudge.ts surfaces this in terminal-state errors as a copy-paste hint (overstory-629f).

Three liveness topologies for AgentSession (overstory-7a34): tmux (tmuxSession !== '' → tmux session must exist), long-lived headless (tmuxSession === '' && pid !== null → isProcessAlive(pid) — coordinator/orchestrator/monitor/sapling), spawn-per-turn (tmuxSession === '' && pid === null → no persistent process between turns; alive iff state non-terminal for UI, time-based only for evaluateHealth). Any new code that gates on 'is this agent alive right now?' MUST handle all three — gating only by 'pid !== null' silently misclassifies the spawn-per-turn fleet.

Coordinator start preflight detects leftover watchdog daemon (overstory-3f0c): src/commands/coordinator.ts startCoordinatorSession() calls watchdog.isRunning() right after watchdog DI resolution, BEFORE deployConfig/spawn, and throws ValidationError when a daemon is running unless --watchdog or --accept-existing-watchdog is passed. --watchdog acknowledges the preexisting daemon (no double-start). JSON output exposes watchdogPreexisting:boolean so callers can distinguish 'we started it' from 'it was already there'. The CoordinatorSessionOptions.acceptExistingWatchdog field threads through startPersistentAgent → createPersistentAgentCommand so orchestrator inherits the same behavior. Web UI start path (serve/coordinator-actions.ts) does NOT pass acceptExistingWatchdog — it surfaces the ValidationError to the API caller, consistent with the spec.

ov sling auto-dispatch from-field resolution: in src/commands/sling.ts buildAutoDispatch(), 'from' resolves as slingerName ?? parentAgent ?? 'orchestrator'. Slinger comes from OVERSTORY_AGENT_NAME env var (?.trim() || null at call site). The distinction matters: --parent describes the new agent's hierarchical parent (used for beacon's 'Parent: X' line and session.parentAgent), while slinger is who actually invoked ov sling. When coordinator slings a lead per agents/coordinator.md without --parent, attribution must come from env, not the parentAgent fallback.
